title: Aswan ⇄ Dendera ⇄ Luxor
summary: Our signature "Long Cruise" — the classic southern route deepened with an exclusive excursion to Abydos and the magnificent Temple of Dendera.
description: A deep exploration of the Nile, combining the classic temples of the south with a rare pilgrimage to Abydos and Dendera — two of Egypt's most sacred and least-visited cult centres, often missed on standard itineraries. The schedule balances iconic sights with serene sailing.
routeOverview: Six nights threading the Nile's great temples, with an exclusive full day ashore at Abydos and Dendera. Sailable in either direction — beginning amid the islands of Aswan, or with the grandeur of Thebes — both reaching the sublime reliefs of the Temple of Seti I and Dendera's painted astronomical ceiling.
insights: Day 5 — Abydos & Dendera is a full-day road excursion (about 1.5 hours each way from Qena to Abydos), requiring an early start. The day-by-day reflects a realistic timeline for this exceptional day of sightseeing.
The inclusion of Abydos makes this a uniquely comprehensive "Long Cruise," offering access to one of Egypt's most sacred and historically significant sites — often missed on standard itineraries.
The sequence of visits, especially on the West Bank, may be arranged by your guide to ensure the best possible experience based on conditions.

routes.label: Aswan → Dendera → Luxor
routes.overview: This 6-night "Long Cruise" offers a deep exploration of the Nile, combining the classic southern route with an exclusive excursion to the magnificent Temple of Dendara. The schedule is designed to make the most of each day, balancing iconic sights with serene sailing.

timeline.title: Embarkation & The Goddess Isis's Sanctuary
timeline.description: Arrive in Aswan and step aboard — your journey begins on the island of Isis.

schedule.text: Arrive in Aswan, board your dahabiya, and settle in.
Visit the beautiful Philae Temple — a motorboat to Agilkia Island reveals this Graeco-Roman complex dedicated to Isis, famed for its elegant columns and picturesque setting.
Docked in Aswan.

timeline.title: The Dual Temple & Ancient Sandstone Quarries
timeline.description: Two great riverside monuments, with serene sailing in between.

schedule.text: Set sail north towards Kom Ombo.
Visit the unique Temple of Kom Ombo, dedicated to two gods — the crocodile-headed Sobek and the falcon-headed Horus.
Continue sailing to the historic site of El Silsila.
Explore the El Silsila quarries — the source of sandstone for Egypt's great temples — with cliff-side chapels and royal stelae carved into the rock.
Sail onwards to Edfu.
Docked in Edfu.

timeline.title: The Falcon God's Citadel & Sailing to Thebes
timeline.description: Egypt's best-preserved temple, then a full day on the water.

schedule.text: Visit the remarkably preserved Temple of Edfu, the largest dedicated to the god Horus — its massive pylon, courtyards, and intact inner halls a highlight of Ptolemaic architecture.
Depart Edfu for a full day of scenic sailing towards Luxor, ancient Thebes.
Docked in Luxor.

timeline.title: Theban Necropolis & Voyage to the Goddess Hathor
timeline.description: The West Bank of Thebes, then a special sail north toward Dendara.

schedule.text: Cross the Nile to the West Bank — the Valley of the Kings, the Temple of Hatshepsut at Deir el-Bahari, and the towering Colossi of Memnon.
A special northward sail to the city of Qena, the docking point for Dendara.
Docked near Dendara.

timeline.title: Pilgrimage to Abydos & The Temple of Hathor
timeline.description: A full day ashore at two of Egypt's most sacred, least-visited temples.

schedule.text: Travel to Abydos — the magnificent Temple of Seti I, with its exquisite raised reliefs and the mystical Abydos King List, and the nearby Osireion.
Return to Qena for the stunning Temple of Dendara, dedicated to Hathor — its famous astronomical ceiling, vivid column capitals, and rooftop chapels.
Set sail back to Luxor.
Docked in Luxor.

timeline.title: The Grand Temples of Thebes
timeline.description: The grand finale, on the East Bank of ancient Thebes.

schedule.text: Stand within the colossal Karnak Temple Complex — the forest of pillars in the Great Hypostyle Hall — then the elegant Luxor Temple on the Nile Corniche.
Docked in Luxor.
After breakfast, disembark in Luxor, concluding your deep journey through Egyptian history and spirituality.

routes.label: Luxor → Dendera → Aswan
routes.overview: This six-night itinerary offers a strategic and enriching route. First explore the wonders of ancient Thebes (Luxor), then embark on a special excursion north to the cult temples of Abydos and Dendara, before a relaxing downriver sail to Aswan — concluding with the beautiful sites of the south.
insights: Strategic route — this direction lets you experience the grandeur of Luxor first, when energy is highest, followed by the special excursion. The upriver sail from Luxor to Aswan can allow for a more relaxed pace.
Abydos & Dendera — the Day 3 excursion is a significant full-day road trip from Qena, requiring an early start, but is uniquely rewarding for those drawn to deep historical and religious sites.
Aswan as a finale — ending in Aswan offers a natural conclusion, with the option to extend your stay toward the temples of Abu Simbel, a highlight of any southern Egypt itinerary.

timeline.title: Embarkation in Thebes & The East Bank
timeline.description: Board in Luxor and begin amid the grandeur of ancient Thebes.

schedule.text: Arrive and board your dahabiya in Luxor.

Explore the East Bank — the colossal Karnak Temple Complex, a city of temples, obelisks, and the forest of 134 columns in the Great Hypostyle Hall — then the graceful Luxor Temple, illuminated at dusk.
Docked in Luxor.

timeline.title: The Theban West Bank
timeline.description: The necropolis of ancient Thebes, before the special excursion north.

schedule.text: Cross the Nile to the West Bank — the Valley of the Kings, the Temple of Hatshepsut at Deir el-Bahari, and the towering Colossi of Memnon.
Docked in Luxor.

timeline.title: Pilgrimage to Abydos & The Temple of Hathor
timeline.description: A full day ashore at two of Egypt's most significant temples.

schedule.text: Travel to Abydos — the magnificent Temple of Seti I, with its exquisite raised reliefs and the mystical Abydos King List.
Return to the Qena area for the stunning Temple of Dendara, dedicated to Hathor — its vividly painted astronomical ceiling, crypts, and iconic Hathor-headed columns.
Sail back south from Qena to Luxor.
Docked in Luxor.

timeline.title: Sailing South via Esna to Edfu
timeline.description: A full day of relaxed sailing after the intensive temple days.

schedule.text: Sail downriver towards Edfu, passing through the Esna Lock — a feat of modern engineering that regulates the river's flow — as rural Egyptian life unfolds along the banks.
Docked near Edfu.

timeline.title: Edfu, El Silsila & Kom Ombo
timeline.description: Three monuments and a final serene sail to Aswan.

schedule.text: Visit the monumental Temple of Edfu, dedicated to the falcon god Horus.
Sail south from Edfu to El Silsila.
Explore the sandstone quarries of El Silsila — the cliff-side Speos of Horemheb and royal stelae carved into the rock faces.
Sail onwards to Kom Ombo.
Visit the unique Temple of Kom Ombo, dedicated to both Sobek and Horus — its riverside setting beautiful in the late-afternoon light.
A final serene sail to Aswan.
Docked in Aswan.

timeline.title: The Island of Isis
timeline.description: Aswan's sacred island and its modern marvels.

schedule.text: Visit the beautiful Philae Temple — a short motorboat ride to Agilkia Island reveals this Graeco-Roman complex dedicated to Isis, famed for its elegant pillars.
On the way back, see Aswan's High Dam, the Old Dam, and the Unfinished Obelisk.
Docked in Aswan.

timeline.title: Disembarkation in Aswan
timeline.description: Farewell to the river — with Abu Simbel within reach.

schedule.text: After breakfast, disembark in Aswan — with the option to extend your stay toward the temples of Abu Simbel.

faq.a: The inclusion of Abydos makes this uniquely comprehensive, offering access to one of Egypt's most sacred and historically significant sites — the Temple of Seti I — alongside Dendera, both often missed on standard itineraries.

faq.q: How demanding is the Abydos & Dendera day?
faq.a: It is a full-day road excursion from Qena (roughly 1.5 hours each way to Abydos) requiring an early start, but it is uniquely rewarding for travellers drawn to deep historical and spiritual sites. Comfortable shoes, sun protection, and water are essential.

Either Aswan to Luxor or Luxor to Aswan — the temples are the same, the order differs. Starting in Luxor lets you experience the grandeur of Thebes first; ending in Aswan opens the option to extend toward Abu Simbel. We tailor the direction to your travel plans.

faq.q: Can the order of visits change?
faq.a: Yes. This is a private, flexible charter and a general framework — your guide may adjust the sequence of visits, especially on the West Bank, to ensure the best experience based on river, lock, and site conditions.
